creator: jhelfman@e-e.com
text:
Hi Olli,

On Mon, Jan 24, 2011 at 01:29:33AM +0100, olli hauer thus spake:
>On 2011-01-23 02:22, Jason Helfman wrote:
>- removed OPTION MYSQL
>  It is the only database at the moment and if deselected install stops with
>this error

Although it is the only database option at the moment, I plan on adding
postgresql very soon, however if no datbase is installed the application
will fail to properly function.

My original port had a database defined check under the check-santity
target, respectively, to ensure there was at least one database defined. I
don't recall the original reason why this was removed, however I do recall I
had a response similar to one I have now.

I will certainly support no databases selected, if you like, however I don't 
believe this is the best route with this application. It will build, but
fail to work.

All other changes make sense to me, and thank you for such a timely response
on bringing this PR back to life.

>
>BUILD SUCCESSFUL
>Total time: 4 seconds
>install: /jasperserverCreate-.ddl: No such file or directory
>*** Error code 71
>
>Stop in /usr/ports/databases/jasperserver.
>*** Error code 1
>
>Stop in /usr/ports/databases/jasperserver.
>
>I've uploaded only the changed Makefile
>http://people.freebsd.org/~ohauer/diffs/PR_150208_jasperserver_Makefile_2011-01-24
>and as bz2 in case the custom DOS2UNIX expression is mangled
>http://people.freebsd.org/~ohauer/diffs/PR_150208_jasperserver_Makefile_2011-01-24.bz2
>
>Additional the small test script for "~/"
>http://people.freebsd.org/~ohauer/diffs/PR_150208_jasperserver_home-test
>
>
>

-- 
Jason Helfman
System Administrator
experts-exchange.com
